title: TLS Handshake
content:
# CN Interview Prep

## Q1: How does HTTP/3 overcome the Head-of-Line (HoL) blocking problem present in HTTP/2?

### Standard Answer
HTTP/2 multiplexes multiple streams over a single TCP connection. However, if a single TCP packet is dropped, the TCP receiver window stalls, blocking processing of all subsequent streams until the missing packet is retransmitted (TCP Head-of-Line blocking). HTTP/3 replaces TCP with **QUIC** (which runs over UDP). QUIC implements stream-level flow control: each stream acts as an independent logical channel, so dropping a packet in Stream A does not stall Stream B.


## Q2: How does the system handle concurrent throughput anomalies under TLS Handshake?

### Standard Answer
The system uses dynamic resource pools and queue-backoff throttles to maintain throughput boundaries, avoiding thread starvation.
